title: NOC 21221 – Business Systems Specialists | GTR Immigration
description: Business Systems Specialists are in strong demand across Canada, helping organizations modernize operations, integrate enterprise systems, and bridge business needs with advanced technology solutions. Classified under NOC 21221 (TEER 1), this occupation qualifies for Express-Entry-aligned programs, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), LMIA-supported work permits, and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) pathways.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.major_group: 212 – Professional occupations in applied sciences (except engineering)

duties: Design and implement customized business-technology solutions
Advise on IT strategy, governance, and service delivery
Act as liaison between business units and technical teams
Test system upgrades and new implementations
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, business administration, or information systems
College diploma in computing (in some cases)
Vendor-specific system certifications or training (preferred by some employers)
job_outlook: This represents a large, immigration-relevant labour market, particularly favourable for candidates with:
additional_information: Demand continues to expand rapidly due to:
Enterprise digital-transformation projectsERP and CRM modernizationCloud-migration programsCyber-governance requirementsData-driven operations
IT consulting firmsGovernment departmentsFinancial institutionsHealthcare systemsTechnology divisions in major corporations
Typical annual salaries range from $48,100–$118,500 CAD, depending on province, experience, and system-specialization.
